# OpenForge

> OpenForge is a free, open-source macOS desktop application that orchestrates parallel Claude Code agents across isolated git worktrees. Built with Tauri 2 (Rust) + React 19 + TypeScript.

OpenForge lets developers run multiple Claude Code AI coding agents simultaneously, each in its own git worktree and branch. One agent fixes a bug while another adds a feature while a third writes tests — all at the same time, all in isolated branches, all visible in a single native macOS window.

Key facts:
- License: MIT (fully open-source, not open-core)
- Platform: macOS 12+ (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Tech stack: Tauri 2.x (Rust backend), React 19, TypeScript, Vite
- No telemetry, no accounts, no lock-in
- Current version: 0.2.5

## Docs

- [Landing page](https://samagra14.github.io/openforge/): Project overview, features, and download links
- [Changelog](https://samagra14.github.io/openforge/changelog.html): Release notes and version history
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/samagra14/openforge): Source code, README, and contributing guide
- [Releases](https://github.com/samagra14/openforge/releases): Download DMG binaries for Apple Silicon and Intel

- [Full LLM context](https://samagra14.github.io/openforge/llms-full.txt): Detailed architecture, features, and technical information
